name: Slackdump
what_it_does: Exports and archives Slack messages, threads, files, and user data locally without requiring admin privileges. Includes an MCP server for AI agent integration with archived data.
use_cases: Archiving Slack workspace conversations before leaving a company
Exporting channel history for compliance or audit purposes
Enabling AI agents (via Claude Desktop) to analyze archived Slack data
Incremental backup of Slack workspaces over time
Searching Slack messages and files locally
not_for: Real-time Slack bot or integration development
Teams needing official Slack export with admin approval
Posting or sending messages to Slack
best_when: You need to extract Slack data as a regular user without admin access, or want to feed historical Slack content to an AI agent for analysis.
avoid_when: Your organization's policies prohibit exporting Slack data, or you are on an enterprise workspace with strict DLP controls that would trigger security alerts.

auth.notes: Uses EZ-Login 3000 or direct browser token extraction. No Slack app creation required. Tokens extracted from user's own browser session.

pricing.notes: AGPLv3 licensed. Free to use. Slack workspace plan may affect what data is accessible.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI-focused tool; MCP error surface not well documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Supports incremental/resumable archives which implies some idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Enterprise workspaces may trigger security alerts when using browser token auth
AGPLv3 license may restrict commercial use of derived works
Token extraction from browser may break if Slack changes session handling

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
